content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660          322-5901         322-4441          322-6444

March 24, 1981

Ralph D. Morrell
825 Newgate Way
Dixon, California 95620

A-81-1147

Dear Mr. Morrell:

This letter is in response to our recent telephone conversation
regarding your reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.

The period covered by your campaign statement:

With respect to the "period covered" by campaign statements
filed by proponents of state ballot measures and committees formed
primarily to support or oppose the qualification of ballot measures,
you are correct that the information Manual and Candidate Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act is in error. The "period
covered" for your campaign disclosure statement runs from the first
day you or your committee received contributions or made reportable
expenditures through seven days (not 28 days) prior to the date when
the statement is due. Your statement is due not later than 35 days
after the date of notification that the "Operation Vote - Switch"
measure failed to qualify (Government Code Section 84200(d)).

You are also correct that the period covered by a proponent's
or qualification committee's statement generally commences on
January 1. However, in a case such as yours, where the qualifica-
tion effort was underway in a previous calendar year the period
covered extends back until the date of the first reportable activity
(Government Code Sections 82013, 82046 and page 14 of the information
Manual).

Definition of proponent:

The term "proponent" is defined in Section 29710 of the Elections
Code as follows:

[The] "proponent or proponents of an initiative or referendum
measure" means the person or persons who submit a draft of a petition
proposing the measure to the Attorney General with a request that he
prepare a title and summary of the chief purpose and points of the
proposed measure. (emphasis added)

Ralph D. Morrell                           -2-                              March 24, 1981


     Section 84200(d) of the Government Code requires that "committees
formed or existing primarily to support or oppose the qualification
of a measure and proponents of a state measure shall file campaign
statements........(emphasis added)". Accordingly, you and the other
two individuals who presented the "written rebuttal" discussed in
Section 3502 of the Elections Code are all considered proponents and
therefore have an obligation to file a campaign statement. However,
as explained below we will permit you and the other two proponents to
file one combined statement along with "Operation Vote - Switch"
Committee to reform voting practices in the Assembly of California.

Correct form for filing your campaign statement:

     The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candi-
dates and committees to file combined or "consolidated" campaign
statements (Government Code Section 84211). Pursuant to this author-
ity the Commission authorizes you and other two proponents of the
ballot measure to file one combined campaign statement with the
"Operation Vote - Switch" Committee. The appropriate form is
Recipient Committee Campaign Statement--Form 420, (enclosed) and you
should indicate on the top of the form that it is being filed as a
"combined proponent/committee campaign statement."

     I hope this letter adequately clarifies your reporting obliga-
tions under the Political Reform Act. If you have further questions
in this regard, please contact the Commission's Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division at (916)322-5662.

     I also appreciate your input on the manual and will take appro-
priate steps to make the corrections and improvements we discussed.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        THOMAS K. HOUSTON
                                        Chairman, FPPC

TKH:bw

Enclosure
